full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Walk me through Algorithm 1 from the FGM paper (Section 2.2) step by step. What does 'most violated d_hat' mean and how is it computed?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified that the most violated d_hat is the feature subset that maximises the constraint violation in the cutting-plane formulation (Eq. 4). Helped me understand why sorting c_j^2 gives the global optimum.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified against Section 2.4 of the paper. The equivalence to sorting c_j^2 is explicitly derived in the paper.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Can I use LinearSVC from sklearn as a simplified proxy for SimpleMKL in the FGM paper? Is this mathematically justified?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed that since all kernels are linear (Section 2.7), LinearSVC on the feature union is a reasonable proxy. Helped me write the justification.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified by re-reading Section 2.7: 'all base kernels are linear, subproblem solved by LIBLinear'. LinearSVC uses the same dual coordinate descent approach.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: In L2-SVM, how are dual variables alpha_i related to margin violations? How can I approximate alpha from a trained LinearSVC?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Explained that under the KKT conditions, non-zero alpha_i correspond to support vectors (margin violations). Used this to justify the margin violation approximation.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Cross-checked against Section 2 of the paper where domain A requires alpha_i >= 0 and sum=1. The normalised margin violation approach satisfies these constraints.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: How should I implement the convergence check for Theorem 2 of the FGM paper? The theorem says stop when d_hat is already in C.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested comparing the selected feature index sets for equality. Used set comparison which I then adapted.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified against Theorem 2 and its proof in Section 2.6. The proof shows that when d^{k+1} in C_k, the algorithm has reached global optimum.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Changed from index array equality to set comparison to handle any ordering of feature indices.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: How should I implement SVM-RFE to match the paper's description in Section 1 and 3.3? The paper says 'remove 10 features at a time when total > 200'.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped structure the iterative elimination loop. I adapted the chunk size to proportional elimination since we have only 34 features.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified against Section 3.3: 'remove 10 features at each time when total > 200, otherwise remove one at a time'. For 34 features we use proportional chunks.
